summary: Backend-only testing for PawFind. Ran 18 pytest cases via the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL covering /api/places/nearby (all/shop/groomer + invalid), /api/places/seed_1 detail, /api/places/{unknown}, /api/reviews/{place_id}, auth guards on /api/reviews, /api/favorites (GET+POST), /api/auth/me, /api/auth/session (invalid), /api/auth/logout, and recursive _id leak check on all responses. 18/18 passed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is single-file ~344 lines, OK for now but consider splitting routers (auth/places/reviews/favorites) as it grows.

Real Google Places path could not be exercised because GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is empty; seed-mode only is verified. Once a key is added, retest /places/nearby and /places/{place_id} with real Google IDs.
POST /api/auth/logout returns 200 even without an Authorization header (no-op). Documented as expected behavior; if stricter semantics are desired, return 401.
Emergent OAuth session creation cannot be auto-tested end-to-end (no programmatic way to mint a valid session_id); only the 401 path is verified.
seed_data_creation: None. Used in-code SEED_SHOPS served by backend when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is empty.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend served via seed data (GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY empty). All 6 seeds (seed_1..seed_6) returned by /places/nearby?category=all (3 shop + 3 groomer). seed_1 detail includes app_reviews [], app_rating null, app_review_count 0, google_reviews [], website, phone. Auth-protected endpoints (POST /reviews, GET/POST /favorites, GET /auth/me) all return 401 without/with invalid Bearer. /auth/session with bogus id returns 401. No _id leakage anywhere. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_pawfind_api.py as regression suite. When testing authenticated flows, you must obtain a real Emergent session_id via Google OAuth in the web preview — cannot be automated headless.
